zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Gant or de Gant於大約1253年出生時，她的父親Gilbert de Gaunt35歲，母親Lora de Baliol34歲。她於大約1272年與William Kerdeston在英國英格蘭奧可福爾艾尤爾梅結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1284年享年32歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可諾維其諾維其 凱德拉爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Gant or de Gant于大约1253年出生时，她的父亲Gilbert de Gaunt35岁，母亲Lora de Baliol34岁。她于大约1272年与William Kerdeston在英国英格兰奧可福爾艾尤爾梅结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1284年享年32岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可諾維其諾維其 凱德拉爾。

nameMeanings.name: Gant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ghent in Flanders, Belgium.
English: from Middle English gaunt, gant ‘slim, slender; drawn, thin and angular’.
North German: probably a variant of   Gans   ‘goose’.

variants.name: Gaunt
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of   Gant  .

variants.name: Gans
meanings: German and Dutch:

from gans ‘goose’, hence a metonymic occupational name for someone who bred or tended geese, or a nickname for someone who wobbled or behaved (foolishly) like a goose, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a goose.
nickname for a healthy or strong person, from Middle Low German gans ‘whole, complete, healthy’.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Gant or de Gant was born about 1253, her father, Gilbert de Gaunt, was 35 and her mother, Lora de Baliol, was 34. She married William Kerdeston about 1272, in Ewelme, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1284, at the age of 32, and was buried in Norwich Cathedral, Norwich, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Gant or de Gant est née environ 1253, son père, Gilbert de Gaunt, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Lora de Baliol, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé William Kerdeston environ 1272, à Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1284, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterrée à Norwich Cathedral, Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Gant or de Gant um 1253 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gilbert de Gaunt 35 und ihre Mutter Lora de Baliol 34. Sie hat um 1272 in Ewelme, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom William Kerdeston geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1284 im Alter von 32 Jahren und wurde in Norwich Cathedral, Norwich, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Gant or de Gant è nata il circa 1253, sua padre, Gilbert de Gaunt, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Lora de Baliol, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato William Kerdeston circa 1272, nel Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1284, all'età di 32 anni, e fu sepolta a Norwich Cathedral, Norwich, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Gant or de Gantが約1253年に生まれた時，父Gilbert de Gauntは35歳，母Lora de Baliolは34歳であった。約1272年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアエウエルメでWilliam Kerdestonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1284年に32歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクノーウィチノーウィチ カスエドラルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Gant or de Gant(이)가 대략 1253년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gilbert de Gaunt(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lora de Baliol(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 대략 1272년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 웰에서 William Kerdeston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1284년 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 노위치 노위치 캐테드럴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Gant or de Gant nasceu aproximadamente 1253, seu pai, Gilbert de Gaunt, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Lora de Baliol, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com William Kerdeston aproximadamente 1272, em Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1284, com 32 anos, e foi sepultada em Norwich Cathedral, Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Gant or de Gant родилась около 1253 г., её отцу по имени Gilbert de Gaunt было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Lora de Baliol, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Kerdeston около 1272 г. в местности ювелм, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1284 г. в возрасте 32 лет и была похоронена в местности норвич кэтедраль, норвич, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Gant or de Gant nació aproximadamente 1253, su papá, Gilbert de Gaunt, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Lora de Baliol, tenía 34 años. Se casó con William Kerdeston aproximadamente 1272, en Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1284, a la edad de 32 años, y fue sepultada en Norwich Cathedral, Norwich, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
